31.reason: When i=0, num[i-1] accesses num[-1], causing undefined behavior and potential infinite loop.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  When i=0, num[i-1] accesses num[-1], causing undefined behavior and potential infinite loop.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: When i is 0, accessing num[i-1] (num[-1]) causes undefined behavior/out-of-bounds access. For N=1, this loop runs with i=0, accessing invalid memory, likely causing a crash or infinite loop/hang leading to Time Limit Exceeded or Runtime Error. The condition should check i > 0 before accessing num[i-1].]

4.reason: missing #include<stdlib.h>; malloc may not be properly declared, leading to potential undefined behavior.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  missing #include<stdlib.h>; malloc may not be properly declared, leading to potential undefined behavior.

29.reason: When i reaches 0, accessing num[i-1] (i.e., num[-1]) causes undefined behavior; for n=1 this can lead to an infinite loop and TLE. The loop should stop at i>=1 to avoid out-of-bounds access.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  loop should run from n-1 down to 1, not 0. When i=0, num[i-1] accesses out-of-bounds memory, causing undefined behavior and infinite loop.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  When i reaches 0, accessing num[i-1] (i.e., num[-1]) causes undefined behavior; for n=1 this can lead to an infinite loop and TLE. The loop should stop at i>=1 to avoid out-of-bounds access.
